stem: A lighthouse stands on the edge of a cliff. From the top of the lighthouse, the angle of depression to a boat at point A on the water is 60 degrees, and the angle of depression to a second boat at point B, farther away in the same line, is 30 degrees. If the horizontal distance between the two boats is 80 meters, what is the height of the lighthouse above the water, in meters?
explanation: Let h be the height of the lighthouse above the water. The angle of depression equals the angle of elevation from the boat to the top of the lighthouse. For the closer boat A, tan(60 degrees) = h / d_A, so d_A = h / sqrt(3). For the farther boat B, tan(30 degrees) = h / d_B, so d_B = h * sqrt(3). The distance between the boats is d_B - d_A = h * sqrt(3) - h / sqrt(3) = h(sqrt(3) - 1/sqrt(3)) = h((3 - 1)/sqrt(3)) = 2h / sqrt(3). Setting this equal to 80: 2h / sqrt(3) = 80, so h = 80 * sqrt(3) / 2 = 40 * sqrt(3).

distractorRationale.A: Correctly sets up tan(60 degrees) = h / d_A to get d_A = h / sqrt(3), but then confuses the horizontal distance d_A with the height h, arriving at d_A = 40 and reporting h = 40
distractorRationale.B: Applies tan(60 degrees) directly to the 80-meter distance between the boats as if 80 meters were the distance from one boat to the base, computing h = 80 * tan(60 degrees) = 80 * sqrt(3) = 80 * sqrt(3)
distractorRationale.C: Computes d_B = h * sqrt(3) correctly as 120 (when h = 40 * sqrt(3)), but confuses d_B with the height h and reports h = 120
distractorRationale.D: Correct: Sets up two equations tan(60) = h / d_A and tan(30) = h / d_B, finds d_B - d_A = 2h / sqrt(3) = 80, and solves for h = 40 * sqrt(3) = 40\sqrt{3}

imageDescription: A vertical line represents the lighthouse of height h on the left. Two points A and B are on a horizontal line (water surface) to the right, with A closer to the lighthouse base and B farther. Dashed lines from the top of the lighthouse to A and B represent the lines of sight. The angle of depression to A is labeled 60 degrees, and the angle of depression to B is labeled 30 degrees. The horizontal distance between A and B is labeled 80 m.
